---
title: "About the Book"
---

# About This Book

![]( <bookcover/bookcover(2).png> ){.book-cover fig-alt="Book cover for GEOMETRY and the Art of Design"}

This textbook is written for anyone who is curious about geometry, especially non-mathematics majors.

Geometry appears in design, architecture, art, nature, and everyday problem solving. I hope you start noticing all the geometry around you! This course highlights those connections while building your confidence with core geometric ideas.

Use this text as your course guide:

- Read through the short concept explanations.
- Use index cards for key terms and formulas. Review those regularly.
- Study worked examples step by step.
- Practice with exercises.
- Connect geometric ideas to visual design.
- Create your own geometric art.

## How To Use This Text

In Chapters 2 through 8, you will see the same flow each time so you always know what to expect:

1. Learning goals so you know what to focus on
2. Key terms and formulas (or one key idea for that chapter)
3. A short mini-lecture or worked example to walk you through the math
4. Practice so you can try the ideas right away
5. Art and design connections to show where geometry appears in the real world
6. A creative assignment, with examples and inspiration to get you started
7. Exercises to build confidence and accuracy
8. Further reading and interactive activities if you want to go deeper

## Suggested Chapter Workflow

Move through each chapter slowly. Take notes, sketch diagrams, and work through each problem step by step.

In the Exercises section, I include an active Google Doc link for FIT students and faculty so you can keep practicing and checking your understanding.

## Learning Mindset

You do not need to be a math major to succeed in this class. I want you to focus on steady growth: read carefully, sketch figures, explain your reasoning, and practice consistently.
